title: Avenula levis
intro: Avenula levis, also known as meadow oat-grass, is a type of perennial grass belonging to the Poaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and exhibiting antioxidant activity. The bioactive constituents of Avenula levis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Avenula levis include teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to treat gastrointestinal problems and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Avenula levis is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is also known as Avenula levis (Holmes) Holub, and its classification is as a member of the subfamily Pooideae. Avenula levis is further classified as a genus within the tribe Poeae. The species Avenula levis is closely related to other species within the Avenula genus. It is native to certain regions of Europe.
